headline: Fraud Losses Stabilize, But AI-Driven Threats Are Eroding Trust
summary: New account fraud surged, and scams became harder to detect in 2025SAN FRANCISCO, April 21, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Javelin Strategy & Research today published the 23rd edition of its Identity Fraud Study, titled “The Illusion of Progress,” independently produced with support from TransUnion, Fiserv, Plaid, and Mastercard, finding that while identity fraud losses have stabilized and scam losses have declined, new account fraud surged, and growing consumer distrust fueled in part by AI is reshap

content.title: Fraud Losses Stabilize, But AI-Driven Threats Are Eroding Trust
content.summary: New account fraud surged, and scams became harder to detect in 2025SAN FRANCISCO, April 21, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Javelin Strategy & Research today published the 23rd edition of its Identity Fraud Study, titled “The Illusion of Progress,” independently produced with support from TransUnion, Fiserv, Plaid, and Mastercard, finding that while identity fraud losses have stabilized and scam losses have declined, new account fraud surged, and growing consumer distrust fueled in part by AI is reshap
